Patient with heart failure with reduced ejection fraction reported worsening orthopnea and leg edema after missing several doses of furosemide. Exam showed bibasilar crackles, and echocardiogram demonstrated reduced ejection fraction.
Computed tomography angiography showed acute pulmonary embolism with right heart strain. The patient was started on apixaban after venous duplex ultrasound confirmed acute deep vein thrombosis.

A patient with poorly controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus presented with foot ulcer drainage and exposed bone. Foot MRI was concerning for diabetic foot osteomyelitis, and bone biopsy was planned before narrowing antibiotics.

Electroencephalogram captured epileptiform discharges during evaluation for generalized tonic clonic seizure. Levetiracetam was started, and seizure precautions were continued because of persistent postictal confusion.
The patient had heat intolerance, fine tremor, and a suppressed TSH with elevated free thyroxine. Endocrinology recommended methimazole and thyroid uptake scan for suspected thyrotoxicosis.

A patient with plaque psoriasis had erythematous plaques on extensor surfaces with silvery scale. Joint pain raised concern for psoriatic arthritis, although no joint erosion was seen on the initial radiograph.

Coronary angiography demonstrated critical coronary stenosis after non ST elevation myocardial infarction. Heparin infusion was continued until percutaneous coronary intervention, and the team monitored for recurrent ischemia.
The patient denied pleuritic chest pain, but computed tomography angiography later showed segmental pulmonary embolus. There was no evidence of right heart strain on echocardiogram.

Researchers used hemoglobin A1c and medication exposure to identify poorly controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus in electronic health records. Elevated albumin creatinine ratio was associated with progressive renal disease during follow up.

A procedure note documented ultrasound guided venous access for central venous catheter placement. The right internal jugular central line was placed using sterile technique, and the team monitored for line associated infection.
Brain MRI showed acute infarct in the right MCA territory after sudden left sided weakness and facial droop. Aspirin and statin were started, but persistent neurologic deficit remained at discharge.

The cohort study compared patients receiving vancomycin for diabetic foot osteomyelitis with patients treated for soft tissue infection alone. Exposed bone and positive bone biopsy were associated with higher amputation risk.
Emergency department evaluation showed fever, dysuria, and flank pain. Urinalysis had nitrites and pyuria, urine culture grew Escherichia coli, and ceftriaxone was given for acute pyelonephritis.

Postoperative patient developed hypoxia with bibasilar infiltrates on chest radiograph. Sputum culture and elevated procalcitonin supported bacterial pneumonia, and oxygen therapy plus ceftriaxone were started.
The oncology note described invasive ductal breast carcinoma with estrogen receptor positive disease. She received lumpectomy followed by adjuvant radiation therapy and tamoxifen.

Rheumatology evaluated morning stiffness and symmetric MCP swelling. Positive rheumatoid factor and anti cyclic citrullinated peptide antibodies supported rheumatoid arthritis, and methotrexate was started.
He presented with hematemesis and melena after heavy NSAID use. Endoscopy found a bleeding gastric ulcer, and pantoprazole infusion was started before transfusion.

After total knee arthroplasty, calf swelling and elevated D-dimer prompted venous duplex ultrasound. Acute deep vein thrombosis was treated with apixaban, and pulmonary embolism symptoms were reviewed.
An ICU patient with septic shock required norepinephrine, broad spectrum antibiotics, and lactate monitoring. Blood cultures later grew methicillin resistant Staphylococcus aureus.
A child with wheezing and increased work of breathing had asthma exacerbation triggered by rhinovirus. Albuterol nebulizers, systemic corticosteroids, and pulse oximetry monitoring were ordered.

After intravenous contrast exposure, creatinine increased from baseline and urine output declined. Nephrology diagnosed acute kidney injury, reviewed urinalysis with granular casts, and recommended isotonic fluids while holding lisinopril.

Electrocardiogram showed ST elevation in the inferior leads with elevated troponin. Emergent coronary angiography found right coronary artery occlusion, and percutaneous coronary intervention with stent placement was performed.

A pregnant patient at 34 weeks developed severe hypertension, headache, and proteinuria. Obstetrics diagnosed preeclampsia with severe features and started magnesium sulfate before induction of labor.
The patient had microcytic anemia with low ferritin and positive fecal occult blood testing. Colonoscopy found a bleeding colon mass, and packed red blood cell transfusion was ordered.
A patient with chronic hepatitis C had elevated transaminases and thrombocytopenia. Liver ultrasound showed cirrhosis with ascites, and hepatology started spironolactone for volume management.
Blood glucose was 38 mg/dL after excess insulin use, and the patient was diaphoretic and confused. Dextrose was given for severe hypoglycemia, and continuous glucose monitoring was reviewed.

A patient with fever, neck stiffness, and photophobia underwent lumbar puncture. Cerebrospinal fluid showed neutrophilic pleocytosis and low glucose, so ceftriaxone and vancomycin were started for bacterial meningitis.

The oncology trial enrolled patients with EGFR mutated non small cell lung cancer. Osimertinib improved progression free survival compared with platinum chemotherapy in participants with brain metastases.
A patient with obstructive sleep apnea reported daytime somnolence and loud snoring. Polysomnography showed an elevated apnea hypopnea index, and continuous positive airway pressure was prescribed.
The patient developed urinary retention after spinal anesthesia. Bladder scan showed 800 mL post void residual, and a Foley catheter was inserted before starting tamsulosin.
A child with fever and barking cough had inspiratory stridor on exam. Neck radiograph suggested croup, and dexamethasone plus nebulized epinephrine were administered.

The transplant recipient developed rising creatinine and decreased tacrolimus trough levels after missed doses. Kidney biopsy showed acute cellular rejection, and high dose methylprednisolone was started.

A patient with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease had worsening dyspnea and wheezing after a viral illness. Spirometry history confirmed obstruction, oxygen saturation measurement was low, and prednisone plus azithromycin were prescribed for COPD exacerbation.

The patient had atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response. Metoprolol was given for rate control, warfarin was adjusted after an elevated INR, echocardiography was ordered, and cardioversion was discussed.
A patient with recurrent migraine reported photophobia, nausea, and unilateral headache. Computed tomography of head was negative, and sumatriptan relieved the acute attack.
Behavioral health evaluated major depressive disorder with insomnia and suicidal ideation. The patient completed a PHQ-9 questionnaire, started sertraline, and arranged close outpatient follow up.

A hospitalized patient developed alcohol withdrawal syndrome with tremor, seizure, and hallucinations concerning for delirium tremens. Lorazepam was administered, and thiamine was ordered before glucose-containing fluids.
Emergency responders found opioid overdose after fentanyl use with coma and respiratory depression. Naloxone improved ventilation, and the patient was monitored for recurrent sedation.

An adolescent with diabetic ketoacidosis had high blood glucose, serum ketones, metabolic acidosis, and low potassium after vomiting. Insulin infusion and potassium replacement were started in the intensive care unit.
Electrocardiogram showed peaked T waves during severe hyperkalemia. Calcium gluconate and sodium zirconium cyclosilicate were given, and nephrology arranged hemodialysis.
The patient developed hyponatremia from syndrome of inappropriate antidiuretic hormone secretion and then had seizures. Sodium was monitored every four hours, and fluid restriction was ordered.
Primary care diagnosed hypothyroidism after fatigue, elevated thyroid stimulating hormone, and low free thyroxine. Levothyroxine was started with repeat TSH testing planned.
After a bee sting, the patient developed anaphylaxis with urticaria, angioedema, and wheezing. Intramuscular epinephrine was administered before transfer to the emergency department.
An inpatient with bacterial pneumonia developed sepsis and hypotension. Blood culture was collected, procalcitonin measurement was elevated, and empiric vancomycin plus cefepime were started.
Exam showed lower leg cellulitis with a fluctuant abscess. Ultrasonography confirmed a fluid collection, incision and drainage was performed, and cephalexin was prescribed after discharge.
Surgery evaluated right lower quadrant pain with fever and leukocytosis. Computed tomography of abdomen showed appendicitis, and laparoscopic appendectomy was scheduled.
A patient had right upper quadrant pain, fever, gallstones, and elevated bilirubin. Ultrasound supported acute cholecystitis, and cholecystectomy was planned.
Heavy alcohol use preceded acute pancreatitis with severe abdominal pain and elevated lipase. The team diagnosed alcohol use disorder and started aggressive hydration and analgesia.
Gastroenterology saw gastroesophageal reflux disease with dysphagia despite omeprazole. Endoscopy showed Barrett esophagus, and long-term acid suppression was recommended.
A patient with Crohn disease developed diarrhea and perianal fistula. Colonoscopy showed active ileitis, and infliximab infusion was restarted.
Rheumatology followed systemic lupus erythematosus with new proteinuria. Kidney biopsy confirmed lupus nephritis, and hydroxychloroquine was continued.
The patient had podagra with swollen first metatarsophalangeal joint and elevated uric acid. Colchicine treated the acute gout flare, and allopurinol was restarted later.
Dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry showed osteoporosis after a vertebral compression fracture. Alendronate and vitamin D were recommended.
After a fall, an older adult had femoral neck fracture and delirium. Orthopedics performed open reduction internal fixation for the hip fracture.
Neurology evaluated Alzheimer disease with progressive memory loss and dementia. The Mini-Mental State Examination score declined, and donepezil was started.
Clinic documented Parkinson disease with resting tremor, bradykinesia, and gait disturbance. Levodopa dose was increased because freezing episodes worsened.
A patient with multiple sclerosis developed optic neuritis and paresthesia. Magnetic resonance imaging showed new demyelinating lesions, and methylprednisolone was given.
The HIV infection visit reviewed viral load, CD4 lymphocyte count, and adherence to tenofovir. Low CD4 count raised concern for Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia prophylaxis.
A patient with tuberculosis had hemoptysis and an abnormal chest X-ray. Isoniazid and rifampin were started while public health arranged directly observed therapy.
Polymerase chain reaction confirmed COVID-19 in a patient with hypoxia. Dexamethasone and remdesivir were started because oxygen requirement increased.
During influenza season, the patient developed fever, cough, myalgia, and a positive rapid test. Oseltamivir was prescribed because symptoms began yesterday.
A hiker had tick bite followed by erythema migrans and facial palsy. Lyme disease was diagnosed, and doxycycline was prescribed.
Urethritis testing was positive for gonorrhea and chlamydia infection. Ceftriaxone was administered in clinic, and azithromycin was prescribed for partner treatment.
Gynecology diagnosed pelvic inflammatory disease after pelvic pain and adnexal tenderness. Doxycycline was started, and ultrasound was ordered to exclude tubo-ovarian abscess.
Urology evaluated benign prostatic hyperplasia with urinary frequency and high post void residual urine volume. Tamsulosin was started, and prostate-specific antigen testing was reviewed.
A patient with vaginal bleeding and pelvic pain had rising beta-human chorionic gonadotropin without intrauterine pregnancy on pelvic ultrasound. Ectopic pregnancy was treated with methotrexate.
Ophthalmology followed diabetic retinopathy with reduced visual acuity and macular edema. Retinal examination showed neovascularization, and laser photocoagulation was performed.
An eye clinic visit found glaucoma with elevated intraocular pressure and optic nerve cupping. Timolol drops were prescribed, and visual field test was scheduled.
The patient had cataract with blurred vision despite new glasses. Phacoemulsification cataract extraction with intraocular lens implantation was performed.
A child with fever and ear pain had a bulging tympanic membrane. Otitis media was diagnosed, and amoxicillin was prescribed.
The patient had sinusitis with nasal congestion, rhinorrhea, and facial pain for two weeks. Amoxicillin clavulanate was prescribed after conservative therapy failed.
Pulmonary hypertension caused progressive dyspnea with tricuspid regurgitation on echocardiography. Right heart catheterization confirmed elevated pressures, and sildenafil was started.
Echocardiography showed severe aortic valve stenosis in a patient with syncope and systolic murmur. Cardiology recommended aortic valve replacement.
Fever and a new murmur raised concern for endocarditis. Blood culture grew Staphylococcus aureus, transesophageal echocardiography showed vegetation, and vancomycin was started.
Nephrolithiasis caused flank pain and hematuria, and computed tomography showed an obstructing ureteral stone. Tamsulosin was given while urology considered ureteral stent placement.
Endocrinology evaluated thyroid nodule with normal thyroid stimulating hormone. Thyroid ultrasound showed suspicious features, so fine needle aspiration biopsy was performed.
Adrenal insufficiency from Addison disease caused hypotension and hyperkalemia. Stress-dose hydrocortisone was administered before discharge teaching.
Celiac disease caused chronic diarrhea and weight loss with positive tissue transglutaminase antibody. A gluten-free diet improved symptoms.
The patient had vitamin B12 deficiency with paresthesia and elevated methylmalonic acid. Cyanocobalamin injections were started.
Sickle cell anemia caused vaso-occlusive crisis with severe pain. Hydroxyurea was continued after discharge.

After an intentional acetaminophen overdose, the patient had nausea, rising transaminases, and concern for acute liver failure. Toxicology started acetylcysteine and monitored the acetaminophen level.
A term newborn developed neonatal jaundice with rising bilirubin and hyperbilirubinemia neonatal on the second day of life. Phototherapy was started, and bilirubin measurement was repeated overnight.
A dental abscess caused facial swelling and severe tooth pain. Dentistry performed incision and drainage, and amoxicillin clavulanate was prescribed.
A scald injury caused a partial thickness second degree burn on the forearm. The wound was dressed with silver sulfadiazine, and tetanus vaccine status was updated.
Dermatology evaluated an irregular pigmented lesion, and skin biopsy showed cutaneous melanoma. Sentinel lymph node biopsy was planned before pembrolizumab treatment.
Hematology diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia after pancytopenia and circulating blasts. Bone marrow biopsy was performed, and induction chemotherapy with cytarabine was started.
Genetic testing confirmed cystic fibrosis in a child with chronic cough, pancreatic insufficiency, and an abnormal sweat chloride test. Ivacaftor was discussed because the mutation was responsive.
After delivery, postpartum hemorrhage occurred because of uterine atony. Oxytocin was administered, uterine massage continued, and blood transfusion was prepared.
Vascular clinic evaluated peripheral arterial disease with exertional calf claudication. Ankle brachial index was abnormal, and cilostazol was prescribed along with smoking cessation counseling.
Psychiatry treated bipolar disorder after a manic episode with decreased sleep and pressured speech. Lithium was restarted, and valproate was considered if mania persisted.
A toddler with pica had lead poisoning after an elevated blood lead level. Public health inspected the home, and chelation therapy was arranged.
Audiology evaluated progressive hearing loss with abnormal audiometry. The patient was referred for cochlear implant assessment after hearing aids provided limited benefit.
Wound care assessed a sacral pressure ulcer with necrotic tissue and surrounding erythema. Wound debridement was performed, and pressure offloading was reinforced.
The ICU team managed acute respiratory distress syndrome with hypoxemia despite mechanical ventilation. Prone positioning improved oxygenation, and low tidal volume ventilation was continued.
Hepatology suspected autoimmune hepatitis after elevated transaminases, positive antinuclear antibodies, and positive smooth muscle antibody. Prednisone was started while liver biopsy was reviewed.
Gynecology evaluated chronic pelvic pain and dysmenorrhea from suspected endometriosis. Diagnostic laparoscopy identified peritoneal implants, and hormonal suppression was discussed.

During admission for acute decompensated heart failure with reduced ejection fraction, the patient developed rising creatinine after intravenous furosemide and contrast CT angiography performed for suspected pulmonary embolism. Brain natriuretic peptide was elevated, echocardiography showed an ejection fraction near 25 percent, and renal ultrasound showed no hydronephrosis. Lisinopril was held while spironolactone and empagliflozin were discussed for guideline-directed therapy after acute kidney injury improved.

Pathology from a lung biopsy showed non small cell lung adenocarcinoma with an EGFR exon 19 deletion, high PD-L1 expression, and no ALK rearrangement. Staging MRI demonstrated small brain metastases, but the patient had no seizures or focal neurologic deficit. Oncology started osimertinib instead of platinum chemotherapy and planned surveillance imaging to assess progression free survival.

After recent clindamycin and ceftriaxone exposure for cellulitis, the patient reported ten watery stools daily with cramping abdominal pain, leukocytosis, and a rising creatinine. Stool nucleic acid testing was positive for Clostridioides difficile toxin gene, while ova and parasite testing was negative. The team diagnosed antibiotic associated diarrhea from C. difficile infection, started oral vancomycin, and avoided loperamide because ileus and toxic megacolon were concerns.

A pregnant patient with systemic lupus erythematosus and prior lupus nephritis presented at 31 weeks with severe range blood pressure, headache, edema, and worsening proteinuria. Complement C3 was low and double stranded DNA antibody was positive, raising concern for a lupus flare, but thrombocytopenia and elevated liver enzymes favored preeclampsia with severe features. Magnesium sulfate and labetalol were started while nephrology and obstetrics debated renal biopsy versus delivery.

A patient with acute ischemic stroke arrived with acute aphasia, right facial droop, neglect, and left gaze preference after being found down at home. Noncontrast head CT showed no hemorrhage, CT angiography showed a left middle cerebral artery large vessel occlusion, and CT perfusion demonstrated salvageable penumbra. Tenecteplase was administered before mechanical thrombectomy, and later telemetry revealed atrial fibrillation requiring anticoagulation with apixaban.

The ICU team treated COVID-19 pneumonia complicated by acute respiratory distress syndrome, septic shock, and acute kidney injury. The patient required low tidal volume mechanical ventilation, prone positioning, norepinephrine, lactate monitoring, and broad spectrum antibiotics while blood cultures were pending. When cultures grew methicillin resistant Staphylococcus aureus, vancomycin was continued and remdesivir was stopped after liver transaminases increased.

After percutaneous coronary intervention with drug eluting stent placement, pharmacogenomic testing showed the patient was a CYP2C19 poor metabolizer with reduced clopidogrel activation. Cardiology switched from clopidogrel to ticagrelor, while infectious disease separately reviewed HIV therapy and avoided abacavir because HLA-B*5701 screening showed hypersensitivity reaction risk. The note emphasized that the pharmacogenomic findings predicted drug response rather than a new diagnosis.

In a randomized placebo controlled trial of adults with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease, semaglutide reduced hemoglobin A1c and body weight while investigators monitored albuminuria, estimated glomerular filtration rate, nausea, and severe hypoglycemia as safety outcomes.

A multicenter cohort study of Crohn disease used colonoscopy, fecal calprotectin, and magnetic resonance enterography to classify active ileitis and perianal fistula before comparing infliximab dose escalation with usual care.

A systematic review of immune checkpoint inhibitor toxicity found that pembrolizumab and nivolumab were associated with immune mediated colitis, watery diarrhea, and corticosteroid treatment; colonoscopy was commonly used to confirm colitis severity.

A pharmacogenomics manuscript reported that CYP2C19 loss of function alleles and poor metabolizer status predicted reduced clopidogrel activation after percutaneous coronary intervention; switching to ticagrelor reduced recurrent ischemic events in the high risk subgroup.

A narrative review described status migrainosus as a disabling migraine attack lasting longer than 72 hours and summarized acute treatment with subcutaneous sumatriptan, nonsteroidal anti inflammatory drugs, acetaminophen, magnesium sulfate, corticosteroids, and dihydroergotamine.

A phase 3 oncology abstract enrolled patients with EGFR mutated non small cell lung cancer and untreated brain metastases. Osimertinib improved progression free survival compared with platinum chemotherapy, while subgroup analyses reported benefit in participants with exon 19 deletion and no baseline seizures.
